feature_inventory.detail: After generating suggestions, a second LLM call scores each 0-10 with rationale, re-ranks, and filters out score=0 items. Score threshold configurable. Solves the problem of LLMs being poor at simultaneous generation and ranking.

Extends hunk context asymmetrically: more lines before change (up to enclosing function/class boundary) than after. Prevents 'needle in haystack' overload while giving enough context to understand the change. Configurable: allow_dynamic_context, max_extra_lines_before_dynamic_context=8.

For large PRs: prioritizes language (most common in repo first), removes deletion-only hunks, uses tiktoken for budget-aware fitting. Additions prioritized over deletions. Remaining patches listed as 'other modified files'. Enables full-repo-aware review without context overflow.

Fetches linked ticket requirements, generates TicketCompliance output: fully_compliant_requirements, not_compliant_requirements, requires_further_human_verification. Requires ticket URL in PR description. branch_issue_regex can extract issue number from branch name.

Reviews only commits added since last review. Configurable thresholds: minimal_commits_for_incremental_review, minimal_minutes_for_incremental_review. Partial port to GitLab in-progress (open PR #2389).

feature_inventory.feature: Structured output schema with confidence constraints
feature_inventory.detail: Pydantic output contract: KeyIssuesComponentLink (file, issue_header, issue_content, start_line, end_line). Prompt explicitly requires: 'do not flag unless you can identify the specific affected code path from the diff context.' Max 3 findings by default (num_max_findings). Security issues may be included with explicit uncertainty noted.

Three config file locations: global, repo-level (.pr_agent.toml), and GitHub wiki page. All merged. extra_instructions field allows per-team customization of review focus. per-file instructions planned in SKILL.md format (open PR #2385).
Open PR #2356 implements MCP tool registry and integration. Open PR #2348 adds MCP orchestration and server config support. Not yet merged as of research date.

feature_inventory.feature: Review risk assessment + repo review rules
feature_inventory.detail: Open PR #2391 adds review risk assessment and optional repository review rules. Not yet merged.
Open PR #2387 adds support for per-repo context files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md etc.) to be included as context. Not yet merged.

Architecture page states: 'Sandboxed cloud execution with full repository cloned for isolated analysis' and 'Agentic exploration that autonomously investigates your codebase for context.' Not just diff-read - review agent can search the full repo.

Team members chat with @coderabbitai to express preferences during reviews. CodeRabbit creates natural-language learning records stored per-org. Applied at every future review comment generation. Scopeable: repo-only or org-wide. Export/import CSV supported. Key insight: preferences tied to file pattern + context of original comment.

Linked repositories configuration. Research agent checks linked repos when PR touches shared APIs, schemas, or types. Findings appear in 'Additional context used' section. Use cases: API contracts, microservice consumers, shared libraries, database schemas. Plan-gated (Pro/Pro+/Enterprise).

Reorganizes diff from alphabetical file list into cohorts (related files grouped) and layers (ordered by dependency: data shapes before consumers). Per-range AI summaries. Auto-generated diagrams for call flows, state machines, ERDs where warranted. Keyboard navigation. Early access, GitHub only. Will become Pro+ feature.

feature_inventory.feature: CI/CD pipeline analysis (post inline fix suggestions on CI failure lines)
feature_inventory.detail: Reads CI/CD pipeline failures and posts inline fix suggestions on the relevant PR lines. CircleCI integration available. Changelog entries confirm shipping.

feature_inventory.feature: 40+ static analysis / linter / SAST tools integrated
feature_inventory.detail: Tools include: ESLint, Ruff, Semgrep, OpenGrep, Betterleaks (secret scanning), TruffleHog, Checkov, Trivy, RuboCop, SwiftLint, Clippy, Brakeman, PHPStan, golangci-lint, ShellCheck, Clang-Tidy, detekt, PMD, PSScriptAnalyzer, yamllint, markdownlint, and 20+ more. All fire during review pass.

Can write structural code pattern rules using ast-grep to give precise, syntax-aware review instructions tied to specific code patterns - not just file globs. E.g. 'when a React component uses useEffect without cleanup, flag it.'
Apply different review instructions to different paths (controllers, tests, docs, etc.) using glob patterns in .coderabbit.yaml. Foundation for codebase-specific reviewer behavior.
Analyzes PRs on public GitHub repos for signals of low-quality AI-generated content. Notes in walkthrough comment. Optional labeling. No extra setup needed - runs as part of standard review pipeline.
Let CodeRabbit implement fixes for unresolved review findings with one click. No copy-paste switching.

feature_inventory.feature: Incremental reviews (focus on what changed since last review)
feature_inventory.detail: 'New commits: Incremental reviews that track what's new since the last review. Every update: Fresh insights without repeating resolved comments.'

feature_inventory.feature: MCP server integration as knowledge source
feature_inventory.detail: Connect external tools as MCP servers to give CodeRabbit additional context from documentation systems, design tools, project management platforms during reviews.
CodeRabbit can use web search to gather additional context during code reviews and chat responses.

feature_inventory.feature: Full codebase semantic search / indexing for PR context
feature_inventory.detail: Marketing claims: 'Merge 4x Faster, Catch 3x More Bugs.' Approach described as indexing full codebase for semantic search, enabling context beyond the diff. No independent user benchmarks found to corroborate the 4x/3x claims. [unverified-vendor-claim]

architecture_inferences.inference: Parallel specialized AI agents: Review agent + Verification agent + Chat agent + Pre-Merge Checks agent run simultaneously, each with full repo clone in sandbox. This explains why CodeRabbit can find cross-repo issues while PR-Agent cannot.

Single-pass with optional two-pass for suggestions. No persistent codebase index - works purely from diff + dynamic context expansion. Faster (30s per review) and cheaper, but fundamentally limited to what's visible in the diff window.

repo_settings_file=true and use_wiki_settings_file=true: team preferences encoded as TOML in .pr_agent.toml checked into the repo. This is the open-source equivalent of CodeRabbit's learnings - but static/manual, not learned from conversation.

key_technical_insight_for_wr: PR-Agent's two-pass self-reflection (generate -> score 0-10 -> re-rank -> filter) is the single most directly adoptable technique. The confidence constraint in their prompt is also directly adoptable: 'Do not speculate that a change might break other code unless you can identify the specific affected code path from the diff context.' CodeRabbit's agentic exploration solves our single-session gap but requires infrastructure we don't have (full repo clone + search). Their learnings system is the closest thing to codebase-specific reviewer families.
